weather_report:
Late morning in Wembley comes under a quilt of broken cloud: soft light, silvered edges, and sudden, welcome gleams of sun. It looks mostly dry and gently bright rather than blazing; the sun will come and go, with a moderate midday UV bite when it finds the gaps, so sunglasses are worth keeping handy. The air feels clear enough for distant outlines to stay sharp, though the day carries that pale, slightly milky cast that softens colors. Expect the sky to keep this on-and-off rhythm through the afternoon.

Dress for changeability: light layers, a breathable shirt with a cardigan or thin jacket you can pocket when the sun warms your shoulders. Good weather for ordinary pleasures—an errand loop on foot, a slow stroll through Wembley Park, a bench break with a book when the light opens, or tending to the balcony plants between sunbreaks. If you’re out near midday, aim for the bright intervals but mind the UV; shade will still feel cool when the clouds drift back.
